title: [InstCombine] Match scalable splats in m_ImmConstant
summary: The generalization strategy involved creating a reusable pattern matcher (`m_ImmConstant`) that safely identifies immediate constants while excluding constant expressions, particularly for scalable vectors. Developers recognized that the original optimization pattern—which relied on detecting splat constants—needed to handle scalable vectors differently due to their runtime-determined size. The patch introduces `immconstant_ty` to check for non-constant-expression splats in vectors, enabling safe folding across both fixed and scalable types. This abstraction allows the same logic to be applied uniformly in transformations like `FoldShiftByConstant`, eliminating redundant code and supporting new test cases with scalable vectors and constant splats. The approach demonstrates how to encapsulate type-specific constraints into a shared utility, making optimizations more robust and extensible.
